seo.title: Building Agentic RAG with LangChain + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build agentic rag with LangChain + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Agentic RAG with LangChain enables autonomous agents to plan queries, retrieve from multiple sources, and synthesize attributed responses, powering advanced search and Q&A systems. However, complex multi-step reasoning introduces risks like hallucinations, infinite tool loops, and inconsistent citations that can erode trust. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure source faithfulness, accurate planning, and reliable outputs in production.

architectureOverview.heading: Agentic RAG Architecture with LangChain
architectureOverview.description:
Agentic RAG extends traditional RAG by using an LLM agent to dynamically plan retrieval queries across multiple vector stores and tools. The agent receives a user query, decomposes it into sub-queries using a reasoning model, executes parallel retrievals from sources like Pinecone, Weaviate, or web search tools, then synthesizes results with proper attribution. This creates a feedback loop where the agent can re-query or refine based on initial retrieval quality.

The core components include a ReAct-style agent executor with custom tools for vector search, query decomposition, and citation tracking. LangChain's RouterChain or multi-retriever setup handles source selection, while output parsers enforce structured responses with source links. Long conversations are managed via memory buffers with context summarization to prevent token overflow.

This architecture shines for complex questions requiring multi-hop reasoning but amplifies risks around retrieval faithfulness and agent stability.

risks.name: Hallucination in RAG retrieval chains
risks.description: Agent fabricates information when retrieved context is insufficient or irrelevant
risks.mitigation: Vex source faithfulness checks against retrieved chunks

risks.name: Tool call loops in agent executors
risks.description: Agent enters infinite retrieval/refinement cycles without converging
risks.mitigation: Vex loop detection and max iteration enforcement

risks.name: Context window overflow in long conversations
risks.description: Accumulated context + retrievals exceed model token limits
risks.mitigation: Vex token counting and context summarization triggers

risks.name: Inconsistent output formatting across chain steps
risks.description: Citation formats vary between planning, retrieval, and synthesis steps

implementation.title: Multi-Retriever Setup
implementation.description: Configure ensemble retriever across multiple vector stores for comprehensive coverage
implementation.code:
from langchain.retrievers import EnsembleRetriever
from langchain_community.vectorstores import Pinecone, Weaviate

pinecone = Pinecone.from_existing_index(index_name="docs", embedding=embeddings)
weaviate = Weaviate(client=weaviate_client, index_name="knowledge")

retriever1 = pinecone.as_retriever(search_kwargs={"k": 4})
retriever2 = weaviate.as_retriever(search_kwargs={"k": 4})

ensemble_retriever = EnsembleRetriever(
    retrievers=[retriever1, retriever2], 
    weights=[0.6, 0.4]
)

implementation.title: Query Decomposition Agent
implementation.description: Agent breaks complex queries into parallel sub-queries for multi-hop retrieval
implementation.code:
from langchain.agents import create_react_agent, AgentExecutor
from langchain.tools.retriever import create_retriever_tool

search_docs = create_retriever_tool(
    ensemble_retriever,
    "search_documents",
    "Searches across company documents"
)

tools = [search_docs]

agent = create_react_agent(
    llm=ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o"),
    tools=tools,
    prompt=hub.pull("hwchase17/react")
)
agent_executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, tools=tools, max_iterations=5)
